Hello there. It's wonderful that you're interested in listening to these sessions. I want you to know that these recordings are made for you, just as you are. They are here to help you with feelings like worry, stress, or big emotions, no matter who you are.

The most important thing is that you feel safe and comfortable. If you are listening, I will speak directly to you. The ideas and exercises we explore can be helpful for anyone who wants to feel more calm and grounded.

If you ever have big or scary feelings, it's always a good idea to talk with a trusted adult in your life about them. They can help you find the best support.

Now, let's begin a session made just for you. Find a comfortable spot where you can relax for the next few minutes. Maybe you're sitting in a cozy chair, or lying down on your bed. Let your body settle in... and if you like, you can gently close your eyes... or simply soften your gaze, looking at something peaceful in the room.

We're going to start by noticing our breath. You don't need to change it... just notice how it feels to breathe in... and to breathe out. Feel the air moving in through your nose... and out through your mouth. In... and out. Good.

Sometimes our minds can feel very busy, like a browser with too many tabs open. That's perfectly okay. It happens to everyone. For now, see if you can just let those thoughts be there... without needing to click on any of them. Just let them float by, like clouds in a big sky... or like leaves floating down a calm stream.

Now, bring your attention to your feet. Feel the socks on your feet... or the feeling of your shoes... or the floor beneath them. Just notice that feeling. Now, gently squeeze all the muscles in your feet... squeeze them tight... and then let them go completely, feeling them become soft and relaxed. Good.

Move your attention up to your legs. Squeeze the muscles in your calves and thighs... hold it for a moment... and then let it all go, feeling your legs become heavy and relaxed. Now, bring your attention to your hands. Make two gentle fists... not too tight... just enough to feel the muscles working. Feel the warmth in your palms... and then slowly, slowly release your fingers, letting your hands rest open and soft.

Now, let's try a breathing exercise together. We'll imagine we are breathing in a calm color... and breathing out a worried color. You can pick any colors you like. Maybe breathing in a cool, calm blue... and breathing out a buzzy, hot red. Let's try it.

Breathe in your calm color slowly... one... two... three... four... Feel it filling up your lungs. And now breathe out your worried color... one... two... three... four... five... Let it all go. Good. Let's do that two more times. Breathe in calm... two... three... four... And breathe out worry... two... three... four... five...

One last time. Breathe in peace... two... three... four... And breathe out any tightness... two... three... four... five... Excellent.

Now, I'd like you to imagine a place that feels very safe and happy to you. It might be a real place you've been... or a place from a story... or a place you completely make up in your imagination. Picture it in as much detail as you can. What do you see around you? Are there trees, or water, or soft blankets? What colors are there? Now, what sounds do you hear in this safe place? Maybe quiet birds, or gentle wind, or soft music. Finally, what does it smell like here? Maybe fresh air, or cookies baking, or rain on grass.

Just rest in this place in your mind for a few moments... knowing you can visit here anytime you need to feel calm... This is your own special spot...

When you're ready, begin to bring your awareness back to the room you're in. Feel the weight of your body where you're sitting or lying. Notice the sounds around you in the room. And when you feel ready, you can gently open your eyes if they were closed.

You did a really great job. Remember, feelings are like weather—they come and they go. You can always use your breath... or your safe place in your mind... to help you find your calm center again. Try practicing this breath and squeeze exercise once today, maybe before bed. You are strong, and you are learning wonderful ways to take care of your feelings. Be kind to yourself. Goodbye for now.

Hello there. It's so nice to meet you. I'm really glad you're here with me today. I want you to know that your feelings are important, and it's okay to have questions about friends and adventures. Today, we're going to talk about staying safe and having fun in ways that feel good for your whole heart.

You know, making new friends is one of the best parts of being a kid. It's so exciting to meet someone who likes the same games or stories that you do. Sometimes, we might meet new friends in lots of different places. And sometimes, our brains get a really big, exciting idea... like a secret adventure. That feeling is totally normal. Lots of kids have big, exciting ideas pop into their heads.

But here's something really important to know. Our brains and our hearts are super smart. They have a special job, and that job is to keep us safe and happy. It's like having a friendly guardian inside you. That guardian helps you know what feels right and what might feel a little wobbly or confusing.

So let's do a fun practice together right now. It's a way to check in with that smart guardian inside you. First, find a cozy spot to sit or lie down. You can even hug a pillow or a stuffed animal if you want. Good. Now, let's take a big, superhero breath in through your nose... feel your belly get big like a balloon... one... two... three... and let it whoosh out through your mouth... one... two... three. Good job.

Now, I want you to imagine your favorite place in the whole world. Maybe it's your bedroom, or a spot at the park, or your grandma's kitchen. Picture it in your mind. See the colors... notice what it smells like there... feel how comfy and safe it feels. Just stay there in your imagination for a moment... This is your safe place. You can visit here anytime you want, just by closing your eyes and taking those superhero breaths.

Sometimes, an idea might feel exciting, but it also makes our safe place feel a little far away. It might make our tummy feel funny, or our heart beat really fast. That's your inner guardian saying, "Let's think about this." A really, really important rule for staying safe and happy is that secret adventures, especially at night or with people we haven't met in person, are not a game for kids to play by themselves. That's a rule for a very good reason—to keep your wonderful, amazing self safe.

So what can we do with that big, exciting feeling? We can turn it into a different kind of adventure. Let's try one. Keep sitting in your cozy spot. Now, imagine your exciting idea is a bright, shiny ball of light in your hands. It's buzzing with energy. We're not going to throw it away... because the feeling is okay. Instead, we're going to give it to a trusted grown-up. In your mind, picture your mom, or dad, a grandparent, or a teacher you really like. See yourself walking up to them and showing them your shiny ball of light. You can tell them, "I have a big feeling or a big idea." Watch how they smile and help you hold it. They might help you turn that idea into a safe, daytime plan, like a playdate with a friend from school. Just imagine that for a moment...

Let's take three more breaths together. Breathe in... one... two... three... Breathe out... one... two... three. Again, breathe in... one... two... three... Breathe out... one... two... three. One last time... breathe in... one... two... three... Breathe out... one... two... three. Wonderful.

If you ever have a big idea or a big feeling that makes your safe place feel far away, your most important job is to talk to a trusted grown-up. You can always, always tell them what's on your mind. They are there to help you with your adventures and to keep you safe. That's their most important job in the whole world.

You are so smart and so strong for listening to your feelings. Thank you for practicing with me today. Remember, your safe place is always right inside you, just a few breaths away. Be kind to yourself. You're doing great.

So let's begin our time together now. Find a comfortable spot to sit or lie down. Maybe you're in your favorite chair. Or maybe you're cozy in your bed. Fill up your belly like a balloon... one... two... three... And let it go out through your mouth... nice and slow... two... three... four. Good. Let's do that one more time. Breathe in the good air... one... two... three... And sigh it out... two... three... four. Wonderful.

Now... I want you to think about your home. Don't try too hard. Just let the first picture pop into your mind. What do you see? Maybe it's the color of your front door. Or the shape of your windows. Maybe it's your own room. Just notice whatever comes first. There's no test here. This is just your mind saying hello to a place you know so well.

I'm going to be quiet for a moment... and you just look at that picture in your mind. See what's there...

Good.

Now... let's think about a sound from your home. What's a sound you hear often? It might be the click of a door closing. Or the hum of the refrigerator. It might be the sound of someone you love laughing. Or the quiet tick of a clock. Listen for that sound in your memory. Just for a few seconds...

Excellent.

Next... let's think about a feeling. What does your home feel like? Maybe it's the softness of your blanket. Or the smooth wood of your table. Maybe it's the cool tile under your feet in the morning. Or the warm sun coming through a window. Remember that feeling for just a moment...

You're doing so well.

Now... here is where we make our story. We're going to take these pieces... the picture, the sound, and the feeling... and we're going to imagine a little guardian for your home. This guardian's job isn't to fight anything. Its job is to notice. To notice all the good, quiet, cozy things about your home. And to remember them.

So in your mind... see if you can imagine what this guardian looks like. It doesn't have to be a person. It could be a friendly animal. It could be a soft, glowing light. It could be a tiny creature made of starlight and dust bunnies. It can look like anything you want. This is your story. What does your home's guardian look like? Take your time to imagine...

Let's give your guardian a name. A simple, friendly name. It could be a real name. Or a made-up name. Maybe it's something like "The Noticer." Or "Keeper of the Cozy." Or "Sam's Helper." Whisper the name in your mind right now...

Perfect.

Now... let's send your guardian on its first noticing mission. In your imagination... watch your guardian float or walk or drift to your favorite spot in your home. Where does it go? Does it sit on your windowsill? Does it curl up in the corner of the sofa? Watch it find its favorite spot...

It might notice the feeling of peace in the air. Your guardian just sits... and notices... and remembers. Let's be quiet and let your guardian do its job for a little while...

Very good.

Whenever you think of your home... you can remember that your guardian is there. Noticing all the little things that make it yours. The sounds. The feelings. The light. Your guardian holds all those memories safe for you.

Now... let's come back to where you are right now. Wiggle your fingers and your toes. Take another big, deep breath in... and let it all the way out. When you're ready... you can gently open your eyes if they were closed.

You have just created a very special story. A story about your home and its gentle guardian. That story belongs to you. You can visit it anytime you like. All you have to do is take a deep breath... and remember your guardian's name... and you can see it again in your favorite spot.

Thank you for sharing your home with me today, Sam. It was a true privilege to visit it through your imagination. Be very proud of the story you created. And as always... remember that the work you do with your counselor is so important. Keep sharing your wonderful ideas with them.

Take good care.
